threats.title: Weak Authentication Bypass
threats.description: Due to a lack of information regarding authentication mechanisms, an attacker could potentially bypass authentication controls, gaining unauthorized access to the system. This could involve exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication process, such as weak passwords, lack of multi-factor authentication, or vulnerabilities in the authentication libraries themselves.
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of vulnerabilities in the authentication logic, credential stuffing, brute-force attacks, or social engineering to obtain valid credentials.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation).
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity.

threats.title: SQL Injection Vulnerability
threats.description: Without knowing the application's data handling practices and input validation, there's a high risk of SQL injection vulnerabilities. An attacker could inject malicious SQL code into input fields, allowing them to manipulate the database, potentially stealing data, modifying records, or even gaining control of the database server.
threats.attack_vector: Injection of malicious SQL code through vulnerable input fields (e.g., search boxes, login forms) that are not properly sanitized or parameterized.
mitigations: Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection.
Employ input validation and sanitization to filter out malicious characters.
Regularly update database software to patch known vulnerabilities.

threats.title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
threats.description: Without knowing the application's authorization mechanisms, there's a risk of IDOR vulnerabilities. An attacker could manipulate URLs or other parameters to access resources or perform actions that they are not authorized to perform, potentially gaining elevated privileges or accessing sensitive data.
threats.attack_vector: Directly referencing internal objects (e.g., database IDs, file names) in URLs or request parameters without proper authorization checks.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all API endpoints and web pages.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct object references.
Regularly review and update authorization logic to address new vulnerabilities.

threats.title: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Lack of Access Controls
threats.description: The absence of information regarding access controls and data classification creates a significant risk of unauthorized access to sensitive data.  If data is not properly classified and access controls are not implemented, attackers could potentially gain access to confidential information, leading to data breaches and compliance violations.
threats.affected_component: All Data Storage Components, Web Application
threats.attack_vector: Exploitation of misconfigured access controls, vulnerabilities in the web application, or direct access to data storage locations.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege.
Regularly audit access permissions to ensure they are appropriate.
Encrypt sensitive data at rest and in transit.

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: The system's vulnerability to denial-of-service (DoS) attacks is heightened due to the lack of information about its architecture and security controls. An attacker could flood the system with requests or inject malicious data that consumes excessive resources, leading to service outages.
threats.affected_component: Web Server, Database Server, Network Infrastructure
threats.attack_vector: Injection of large or complex data, overwhelming the server with requests, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the application code to consume excessive resources.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to restrict the number of requests from a single source.
Employ input validation to prevent the injection of malicious data.
Utilize a Content Delivery Network (CDN) to distribute traffic and mitigate DDoS attacks.

metadata.error: ReportRepository.create() got an unexpected keyword argument 'format'

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,auth_service,database threat

semgrep.config_file:
rules:
- id: security-custom-1
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Weak Authentication Bypass
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: SQL Injection Vulnerability
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-89
    owasp: A03:2021
    threat_category: Tampering
  patterns:
  - pattern: cursor.execute($QUERY)
    pattern-not: cursor.execute($QUERY, $PARAMS)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-3
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-269
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Elevation of Privilege
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-4
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Exposure of Sensitive Data due to Lack of Access Controls
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-200
    owasp: A01:2021
    threat_category: Information Disclosure
  patterns:
  - pattern: print($SENSITIVE)
  - pattern: console.log($SENSITIVE)
  severity: ERROR
- id: security-custom-5
  languages:
  - python
  - javascript
  - typescript
  message: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
  metadata:
    category: security
    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-400
    owasp: A05:2021
    threat_category: Denial of Service
  patterns:
  - pattern: eval($X)
  - pattern: exec($X)
  severity: ERROR
